FR-001.title: Eval scores spec.md/plan.md/tasks.md against per-artifact WARN/FAIL budgets defined in one place

FR-002.title: Budget defaults derived from real completed specs (484/509/510) so healthy runs pass with headroom

FR-003.title: Timing marks a step span trusted only on ordered extension-stamped start/complete pair; untrusted is reported

FR-004.title: Burst-journaling (>=3 ai finishes within 1s) FAILs; out-of-band step duration WARNs

FR-005.title: Static prompting check: never-halts roster has no user prompts, clarify asks, negation-safe, missing roster file fails loud

FR-007.title: CI capture-suite runs the eval strict over specs 509/510 fixtures and the command dir

FR-008.title: Stdlib unittest coverage incl. one failing-direction test per dimension

FR-009.title: Docs land with the change: SKILL.md, speckit-extension CHANGELOG [Unreleased], capture-and-timing note

context: constraint: eval is dev tooling, not shipped in either extension package
constraint: speckit-extension changes document in its own CHANGELOG [Unreleased], no version bump
intent: Give the Companion command family a deterministic quality gate: flag verbose artifacts, wasted/untrusted time, and wrong prompting before they ship
expectations: No AI-driven replay of commands or live LLM scoring
No bench-runner orchestration changes (bench integration is a follow-up)
Out-of-band step duration is WARN-only, never a hard gate
approach: A stdlib-only check_quality.py sibling to check_capture.py: verbosity budgets (single BUDGETS table, WARN/FAIL bands calibrated on 484/509/510), timing-waste checks over history[] (trusted boundaries via the deterministic-writer set, 1s burst FAIL, median-relative outlier WARN), and a fence-skipping negation-aware prompting scan over an enumerated never-prompt/must-ask roster. Tests ride the existing unittest discovery; CI adds three strict steps to capture-suite.

decisions.decision: Checker lives in the eval skill dir, tests in speckit-extension/tests via sys.path insert
decisions.why: eval family already lives there and ships in neither package; CI unittest discovery needs zero wiring
decisions.rejected: speckit-extension/scripts (packing-list implications) and bench (Node harness, different job)

decisions.decision: Enumerated prompting roster, not prefix-derived, with loud FAIL on a missing roster file
decisions.why: never-prompt is per-command semantics, and a silent skip shrinks the scan surface (the #432/#455 drift class)
decisions.rejected: deriving the roster from extension.yml description prose

decisions.decision: WARN tier never affects exit code, even under --strict
decisions.why: budgets and duration outliers are judgment calls; a flapping CI gate would get deleted
decisions.rejected: strict-warn escalation flag (nothing needs it yet)

decisions.decision: Burst check is absolute (>=3 ai finishes within 1s = FAIL), complementing check_capture task-cadence-span (relative 5%)
decisions.why: the pre-#509 bug shape is a one-instant dump regardless of step length
decisions.rejected: reusing only the relative span check

plan.summary: Three-dimension stdlib checker beside check_capture.py, tests in the existing python suite, three strict CI steps
plan.key_finding: A bare ask pattern matches tasks — word-bounded phrases plus fence-skipping and a negation window are what make the prompting scan pass honestly on main

tasks.summary: 9 tasks in 7 waves: skeleton -> verbosity+timing / prompting (parallel) -> tests -> CI -> docs pair -> validate

implement.summary: check_quality.py (3 dimensions, WARN tier) + 23 tests + strict CI step over 509/510/commands + docs; all gates and suites green

T001.did: check_quality.py skeleton: Report with WARN tier, CLI (--feature-dir/--commands-dir/--json/--strict), exit-code model, single-place thresholds/rosters

T002.did: Verbosity dimension: BUDGETS-table scoring of spec/plan/tasks with WARN/FAIL bands; absent/tiny artifacts never flagged

T003.did: Timing dimension: trusted-boundaries WARN, 1s burst FAIL, median-relative outlier WARN; unreadable/empty history reported not scored-clean

T004.did: Prompting dimension: enumerated rosters, fence-skip + negation window, loud FAIL on missing roster file; passes on main, planted prompt FAILs

T005.did: 23 stdlib unittest cases: healthy pass + failing-direction per dimension, negation/fence/word-boundary hygiene, strict/JSON CLI semantics

T006.did: CI capture-suite gains a strict command-quality step over specs 509/510 and the shipped command sources

T007.did: SKILL.md gains the quality-eval area + run step; capture-and-timing doc notes the quality sibling and its timing assertions

T008.did: speckit-extension CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry in user voice; no version bump

T009.did: Validation green: 489 python tests, 1726 jest tests, compile clean, shape-parity/assemble/manifest/emissions OK, eval strict-passes 509+510+commands

warnings: package-manifest failing-direction test prints its expected refusal to stdout — benign

verified.result: 509: 6 pass/0 warn/0 fail; 510: 6 pass/0 warn/0 fail; prompting: 12 pass/0 fail; strict exit 0

last_action: all 9 tasks done — 489 python + 1726 jest tests pass, fixtures score clean
